circular_title: GST Advisory: New Invoice Management System (IMS) - April 2026
circular_summary: Invoice Management System mandatory from April 1, 2026 for all GST filers.
actions: Review supplier-uploaded invoices within the IMS workflow before auto-population into GSTR-2B and take necessary actions (accept, reject, or keep pending) within the prescribed cycle
Reconcile invoices, debit notes, and credit notes against books before finalising monthly GSTR-3B returns to ensure accurate input tax credit reflection
email_subject: Compliance Advisory: GST Advisory: New Invoice Management System (IMS) - April 2026
email_body:
{
  "subject": "GST Advisory: New Invoice Management System (IMS) - Action Required",
  "body": "Dear Suresh Pillai, 

As the proprietor of Spice Garden Restaurant, a food and beverage establishment, you are required to comply with the new GST Invoice Management System (IMS) to ensure accurate input tax credit reflection and seamless GST return filing. This applies to your business specifically as it involves regular transactions with suppliers that need to be reconciled and reported under the GST framework.

To comply with the new regulations, please take the following actions:
1. Review supplier-uploaded invoices within the IMS workflow before auto-population into GSTR-2B and take necessary actions (accept, reject, or keep pending) within the prescribed cycle.
2. Reconcile invoices, debit notes, and credit notes against books before finalising monthly GSTR-3B returns to ensure accurate input tax credit reflection.

The deadline for compliance is 2026-04-20. Although the penalty for non-compliance is not specified, it is essential to adhere to the deadline to avoid any potential risks.

Please do not hesitate to contact us if you require any assistance with the implementation of the new IMS or have any questions regarding the compliance requirements.

Best regards,
Compliance Advisory Team"
}
internal_notes: CA notes — deadline was estimated based on standard GST filing cycle, cross-checks needed for accurate input tax credit claims
